meta.title: Nakadomari Miyanozawa Ski Area
meta.description: 青森県中泊町 — 津軽鉄道ストーブ列車×雪国体験。

hero.title:
Nakadomari Miyanozawa
Ski Area
hero.tagline: 津軽鉄道ストーブ列車×雪国体験。

live.subtitle: Check Abashiri City official portal
live.updatedAt: Confirm snow & lifts before you go

snow.label: Snow quality
snow.note: Extreme cold · use weather forecasts too

sanctuary.title:
Lakes and sea below,
a Tento hideaway.
sanctuary.lead: In the heart of Abashiri Quasi-National Park, terrain shaped by Mt. Tento and Mt. Taikan. Silver Lake Abashiri, Lake Notoro, and the Okhotsk Sea in one view—a local hill loved by eastern Hokkaido travelers and residents, not a mega resort.
sanctuary.noticeBody: No standalone official website—hours and operations are on the Abashiri City portal. There is no live snow API; check the city site and local weather forecasts before you travel. Operated by Nichisenren Okhotsk Abashiri (designated manager).
sanctuary.imgAlt: Lake Abashiri and the Okhotsk Sea from Mt. Tento—a 360° northern panorama

primary.title: Sea views on Course 2, longest on Course 5
primary.lead: Course 1 is wide and gentle for beginners. Course 2 shifts from mellow to steep with Okhotsk Sea panoramas. Courses 3–5 run through forest—Course 5 is the longest, with chances to spot red foxes. Course 6 is a gentle off-lift slope for sledding and kids (summer venue for Hana-Tento).
primary.imgAlt: Forest runs and Okhotsk views—six varied courses

secondary.title: Hands-free, still affordable
secondary.lead: Ski set adult ¥2,500/day, child ¥2,000. Snowboard ¥1,000/hour; wear adult ¥3,000, child ¥1,500. Sled ¥300/day—low barrier even without your own gear.

bundle.title:
Adult ¥2,940/day,
Wed ¥600 for 2h.
bundle.lead: Season roughly mid-January–mid-March. Mon–Sat 10:00–21:00 (night skiing after 18:00); Sun/holidays 9:00–17:00. Adult day pass ¥2,940, child (junior high and under) ¥1,680. Parent–child 4-hour set ¥2,200; Wednesday special 2-hour ticket ¥600 (sold until 16:00)—among the lowest lift prices in the region.
bundle.imgAlt: Center house and base parking—a quiet local hill

wellness.title:
Then crab
and hot springs.
wellness.lead: The slope cafeteria serves ramen, udon, soba, curry rice, pork bowls, and snacks. Nearby: hokke set meals at Prison Cafeteria, oyster soba at Soba Kiri On. Lakeside Hotel Abashiri Kohanso and Abashiri Kanko Hotel offer snow crab, Shiretoko beef, and onsen for a full Okhotsk evening.
wellness.leadStation: About 900 m (12 min walk) from Kohanso bus stop to the slopes—strong lodging base.
wellness.imgAlt: Okhotsk crab and lakeside baths—classic après-ski

tour.title:
Slot half a day
into eastern Hokkaido.
tour.lead: Add a few hours of skiing to drift-ice tours or museum visits. Base at Memanbetsu Airport or Abashiri Station, drive to the hill, then crab and onsen at a lakeside hotel—satisfaction on a different axis from mega resorts, at striking prices.

01.title: Abashiri Station · Memanbetsu
01.body: ~10 min by bus/taxi from station · ~40 km from airport

02.title: Arrive at slopes
02.body: 428 free parking spaces · 12 min walk from Kohanso stop

ecosystem.title: Six courses & Mt. Tento panorama
ecosystem.body: One 900 m pair lift serves six courses—from beginner practice to forest long runs and sea-view pitches. ~150 base + ~278 summit parking spaces, free; night skiing until 21:00. A compact hill that competes on views and snow in the Okhotsk cold.

access.title: 6 km from Abashiri Station · ~4.5h from Sapporo
access.body: ~6 km from JR Abashiri Station, ~10 min by bus or taxi. From Sapporo via Asahikawa-Kita IC and Route 39, ~200 km (4.5–5 hours). Rental car from Memanbetsu Airport works well for Abashiri–Akan–Shiretoko loops.

green.title: Hana-Tento in summer, ski in winter
green.body: The gentle off-lift slope of Course 6 hosts Hana-Tento in summer—Mt. Tento panorama year-round. In winter, extreme-cold Okhotsk powder and low prices make it a hideaway for local families and eastern Hokkaido travelers.

access.note: ~6 km from JR Abashiri Station · ~40 km from Memanbetsu Airport · 428 free parking · ~900 m walk from Kohanso bus stop
access.places: 北海道Hokkaido中泊町市Abashiri字呼人Yobito28-3
access.rentacarLink: Book a rental car at Memanbetsu Airport
access.rentacarHint: For Abashiri, Akan, and Shiretoko drives

hero.title:
From the hill
to Okhotsk flavors.
hero.tagline: Center house to prison cafeteria & buckwheat

nearbyFood.notice: Hours and menus are on each venue's official site.
nearbyFood.introLead: From slope noodles to seafood and soba—four stops that fit a half-day plan.
nearbyFood.introBody: Lunch on the hill; dinner at lakeside hotels or town favorites.
nearbyFood.spotsLead: From the center house into Abashiri town, in route order.

slope.title: Center house cafeteria
slope.lead: Warm ramen, udon, and soba, plus curry rice, pork bowls, and snacks like meat buns and fries—convenient slope-side refueling.

prison.title: Prison Cafeteria (Abashiri Prison Museum)
prison.lead: Famous for Abashiri prison soba. Hokke and other set meals—pair history and food after skiing.

soba.title: Soba Kiri On
soba.lead: Near Abashiri Agricultural University. Signature oyster-loaded 100% buckwheat soba (fine and country styles)—Okhotsk sea and farm on one plate.

crab.title: Hotel Abashiri Kohanso · Kanko Hotel
crab.lead: Dinner features snow crab courses and Shiretoko beef—reward yourself right after the slopes.

nearbyOnsen.notice: Fees and hours are on each facility's official site.
nearbyOnsen.introLead: Hot springs line Lake Abashiri—crab and bath after skiing is this hill's signature loop.
nearbyOnsen.introBody: Kohanso near the bus stop; Kanko Hotel on the heights—two lakeside picks.
nearbyOnsen.spotsLead: Recovery and lodging bases after skiing.

kohanso.title: Hotel Abashiri Kohanso
kohanso.lead: Spacious rooms and private family baths. Crab-focused dinner courses. ~900 m walk from Kohanso bus stop to slopes—excellent ski base.

kanko.title: Tento-no-Yado Abashiri Kanko Hotel
kanko.lead: Elevated location with fine views. Snow crab courses and Shiretoko beef—full Okhotsk flavor.

hero.title:
Sleep lakeside,
or hub at the station.

corridorStay.notice: Rooms, rates, and shuttles are on each property's booking site.
corridorStay.introLead: Stay at Kohanso and walk to the hill, or use Abashiri Station as your eastern Hokkaido hub—choose by what your trip is about.

kohanso.tag: 12 min from bus stop · crab · private bath
kohanso.title: Hotel Abashiri Kohanso
kohanso.lead: Closest lakeside lodge. Walk from the bus stop; dinner and onsen complete a half-day ski trip—families and couples.

kanko.title: Tento-no-Yado Abashiri Kanko Hotel
kanko.lead: Choose for views and dining. Crab, Shiretoko beef, lakeside baths—the core of a ski-plus-sightseeing night.

station.title: Hotels near Abashiri Station
station.lead: ~6 km to slopes, ~10 min by bus or taxi. Start point for drift ice, museums, and eastern Hokkaido loops—drive or transit to the hill.
